search_queries_used: site:rcoa.ac.uk GPAS regional block follow-up mobilization discharge education 2025
site:asahq.org regional anesthesia follow-up neurologic symptoms documentation
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Resolution, motor and sensory findings, and mobilization must be actually assessed.
Neurological symptoms require direct clinical assessment and cannot trigger an automated diagnosis or referral.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Patients after a regional anesthesia technique requiring clinician-led follow-up.
setting_applicability: Recovery, ward, day-surgery, telephone, or outpatient follow-up where the responsible service performs and documents review.
UAE_applicability: International RCoA guidance requires UAE follow-up ownership, escalation, discharge, and facility-policy adaptation.
recency_verification: Current RCoA GPAS 2025 chapter was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: The current 2025 RCoA GPAS regional-anesthesia chapter was used.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCoA recommendations support documenting follow-up after regional anesthesia, return of function, mobilization assessment, discharge process, education, and already arranged contact or review without asserting a normal block course.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization status, discharge information, and postoperative education without directing management.

unresolved_source_gaps: Technique, site, time, expected duration, pain, sensory and motor function, weakness, mobility, falls, bladder function, catheter, wound, infection, systemic symptoms, examination, complication, action, response, escalation, discharge, and follow-up timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
